behavior_summary: Provides two avatar-facing handlers: AVATAR::use toggles an item's UI (gump) based on an item status bit; AVATAR::look presents a developer/demo menu that can enable cheat/demo features, prompt the player for inputs, spawn helper scripts, and create items around a targeted location.

AVATAR::use: reads the item's status flags (Item::getStatus) and either opens the item's gump (Item::openGump(item,1)) when a specific bit (0x0100) is set, or closes it (Item::closeGump) when not set.

AVATAR::look: interactive debug/demo menu. It checks AvatarCanCheat(), can call MakeAvatarACheater(), plays feedback sounds, builds string menus (Item::ask), collects numeric inputs (Item::getSliderInput), asks the player to pick a target (process target) and obtains coordinates (Item::getX/Y/Z), then attempts many Item::legal_create calls (with coordinate offsets computed by FREE::1A96 and orchestrated by other FREE::* helpers) to spawn items/fragments around that target. It sets demo-related global flags (afterDaemonScene, MalchirTest, malchirMet) for specific demo flows.
notable_control_flow: AVATAR::use is a simple conditional toggle: call Item::getStatus(item); if (status & 0x0100) != 0 then call Item::openGump(item, 1) else call Item::closeGump(item).

AVATAR::look gates demo flows on AvatarCanCheat(); if not a cheater it may flip afterDaemonScene and call MakeAvatarACheater(), then continues to show a multi-entry menu (Item::ask) driving branches like 'Create', 'Demo', 'EndGame', and 'Malchir Death'.

Create/EndGame branches collect numeric inputs with Item::getSliderInput, request a target with process target, read target coords (Item::getX/Y/Z), compute offsets (FREE::1A96), and perform many Item::legal_create attempts; failures are reported via Item::bark.
AVATAR::look frequently spawns other scripts/processes (spawn) and calls FREE::* helpers to manage complex placement patterns; many call results are checked and can abort or continue creation loops.
open_questions: What exact semantic meaning does the item status bit 0x0100 have (inferred to indicate 'gump open' or 'UI-ready' but not explicitly named)?

What are the precise behaviors and algorithms inside FREE::1A96 and FREE::24A7 (they appear to compute coordinate offsets and orchestrate spawn patterns but their exact outputs and failure conditions are unknown)?
Are any persistence or permission side-effects beyond MakeAvatarACheater() set by AVATAR::look (e.g., does enabling cheat mode persist across sessions or affect other subsystems)?
Which specific item type ids and frame numbers are expected/valid in the Create flows (menu obtains these from the player but acceptable ranges/validation are not fully documented here)?

main_function.name: U8P_AVATAR::use
main_function.purpose: When the player uses/activates an item, toggle its UI gump: open the gump if the item's status bit 0x0100 is set, otherwise close the gump.
entry_conditions: Event: U8P_AVATAR::use (player activates/uses an item). The item/process instance is passed as the function parameter.

function_map.name: U8P_AVATAR::use
function_map.role: Handle the avatar 'U8P_AVATAR::use' event for an item; test status flags and open/close item UI (gump).

function_map.name: U8P_AVATAR::look
function_map.role: Interactive developer/demo menu for the avatar: presents choices, gathers input, spawns scripts, toggles cheat/demo flags, and creates items around targets.

external_calls.purpose: Read the item's status flags in order to decide whether its gump should be opened or closed.
Open the item's UI/gump (called with parameter 1 when status bit 0x0100 is present).
Close the item's UI/gump when the status bit 0x0100 is not present.
Check whether the avatar already has cheat/demo permissions to decide menu options and flows.
Grant cheat/demo permissions to the avatar when the demo menu requests it.
Play feedback sound(s) when entering the menu or toggling cheat/demo state.
Display prompt and feedback text to the player for menu labels, errors, and numeric prompts.
Present a list-based menu to the player and receive the chosen option (string).
Collect numeric parameters (frame, quality, quantity, guardian selection) from the player via slider dialogs.
Request the player to pick a target object/location; returned process/item used as anchor for item creation.
Read the X coordinate of the selected target for placement calculations.
Read the Y coordinate of the selected target for placement calculations.
Read the Z/plane of the selected target for placement calculations.
Compute or apply an offset to coordinates before each Item::legal_create call (used to place multiple pieces/fragments around target).
Attempt to create/place an item of a given type at computed coordinates; called repeatedly to spawn fragments/created items.
Higher-level spawn/placement helper used to coordinate complex creation patterns or checks before/after Item::legal_create calls.
Start auxiliary scripts/processes (demo tasks or helper routines) asynchronously from the menu flow.

reads.name: afterDaemonScene
reads.likely_effect: Gate whether a post-daemon demo action should run (prevents re-running the same demo action if already set).

writes.name: afterDaemonScene
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 to mark that the after-daemon demo action has been triggered (prevents re-triggering).

writes.name: MalchirTest
writes.likely_effect: Written when 'Malchir Death' demo option is chosen; likely drives demo/test sequences related to Malchir.

writes.name: malchirMet
writes.likely_effect: Flag set when Malchir-related demo action is invoked to mark progress in the demo/test flow.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides two avatar-facing handlers: AVATAR::use toggles an item's UI (gump) based on an item status bit; AVATAR::look presents a developer/demo menu that can enable cheat/demo features, prompt the player for inputs, spawn helper scripts, and create items around a targeted location.
